# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

parameters:
- name: locationName
  type: string # Location of your Azure SQL Database (example: West US, South Central USA)
  
variables:
  adminLoginUserName: 'sqladmin'
  passwordHashAdminPassword: ''
  sqlDatabaseServerKeyVaultUri: '/subscriptions/your-azure-idhere/resourceGroups/{location}/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/{name}' # Fill this in with your Key Vault URI, replace {...} placeholders by the actual values you want
  sqlDatabaseServerNameSuffix: uniqString(resourceGroup().id) // Unique resource group id for Azure SQL Database Server name suffix  
    example : 'sqladminuniqstring' which is dynamically generated. Use it to avoid conflicts with other deployments in the same subscription/location and Resource Group of this ARM template  or you can use a static string, but don’t forget that each time new deployment will require another uniString
   developmentIPs: ['10.245.98.76', 'your_other_ip'] // Add the IP addresses here for your application's developers/testers  - multiple entries allowed as per requirement, example : [...], if you want to allow connections from any Azure service then use []
    #example: ['*'], which will add all ip range and it can be used in a loop or condition check. However we do not recommend using '*' because IP address conflicts may occur between developers of different applications running on the same VM instance 
   resourceGroupNameDB : '{location}/providers/Microsoft.Sql/servers/{serverNameSuffix}' // Fill this template with your SQL server details, replace {...} by actual values you want    example: '/subscriptions/your-azure-idhere/resourceGroups/{groupname}',
   databaseNameDB : '{uniqueString()}{suffix}.database.windows.net' ‘// Unique resource group id for Azure SQL Database name suffix,example:'uniqstring'.database.windows.net’ // Fill this template with your unique string and .database part of the serverFQDN
   locationDB : 'West US;eastus';           {location} example:west us or east u s – these are used for resource group, database etc if they get deleted then resources in that groups will be disposed as well.    If you want to use same physical-server/grouping of azure services and databases just add them here like 'West US;eastus'
   serverNameSuffixDB : uniqString(resourceGroup().id) // Unique resource group id for Azure SQL Database Server name suffix, replace {...} by actual values you want  example: uniqueuuidstring',    database….databasepartofserverfqdn and location..location part of the FQDN
   firewallNameDB : '{resourceGroup}/servers/{instanceName};FirewallRule' // Your resource group ID for SQL server name suffix, replace {...} by actual values you want example: '/subscriptions/your-azure-idhere/resourceGroups/{groupname}' ‘firewallrule’
   firewallAllowAllTraffic : 'True';           Firewall settings. All connections are allowed True or False depending on requirement; if false, then need to provide list of ips for connection allowlisting in IP address range format (CIDR notation) like "10.245.*.*;yourIP", else all ip addresses can be used
   firewallAllowIpAddress: ['*'] // This should contain your allowed public IPS here if you want to use '*.', it will allow from any IP address and this is for development & testing purposes, multiple entries are accepted. ‘[]’ or just one single entry of ip addresses can be used
   firewallStartIpAddress: ''             // This should contain the starting point in case an existing network has a range then set that else leave it as blank if no specific IP address is required for allow list like '10.245.*' ‘'' to disable connection from ip addresses (firewalls)
   firewallEndIpAddress: '' // This should contain the ending point in case an existing network has a range then set that else leave it as blank if no specific IP address is required for allow list like '10.245.*' ‘'' to disable connection from ip addresses (firewalls)
   adminLoginPassword : "AzureSQLAdmin#$%&/()=?{}" // Auto generated password in case of use or change it manually if you want, leave as comment unless otherwise required for security reasons. The above value is auto-generated using the template parameter and can't be set explicitly to maintain a secret
   databaseSkuName : 'Standard_S0';           Database sku name example: Standard S0 which represents 1 DTUs = 5 core(s) per week upto maximum of 2,496 cores for Free tier (up until and including multi-model concurrent workers capability). For more details please visit azure.microsoft.com
   databaseName : '{uniqueString()}' // Auto generated name in case use or change it manually if you want the above value is auto set to a unique string but not editable, unless otherwise required for security reasons. ‘''to maintain an immutable id and must be between your legal upper bound (100 characters)
   databaseEdition : 'Single' // Single as of now; Multi-model supports in future if needed by enabling it at setup time using below parameter example: Multimodel Support is enabled with multiple model capability. The above value could also include the word "MultiModel" and support for multi_model feature will be available
   databaseVCoreName : 'S0' // S1, P1 or M1 depending on your requirement which represents cpu(s) per week upto maximum of 24 DTUs. example: s5 - multiple model capabilities supported with both core and memory models at once while for single the word will be used
   location : 'West US;eastus' // This should contain resource group, database etc if they get deleted then resources in that groups are disposed as well.. ‘''to maintain a specific physical-server/grouping of azure services & databases. If you want same server or DB for multiple apps just add it here like 'West US;eastus'
   instanceName : uniqString(resourceGroup().id) // Unique resource group id as name suffix example: uniqueuuidstring’ ‘example:'uniqueuid string from above variable can be used to use in below template only once deployment. Once deleted then reuse same subscription and location but different ARM parameters'
   firewallAllowAllIps : 'True';            This parameter is set based on requirement if you want the allowed IPs are all ips, else provide them using this setting (allow list) example: Firewalls settings allow from specific ip addresses ('*') which can be used for staging or test.
    developmentIPRangeListInFirewallSetting : '' // This should contain a comma-separated string of IPS to use in case IP address range, else leave it as blank if no required ‘''if there is any single entry then allow all ips will not work due security reasons’ example: '10.245.*'
   firewallEndIpAddressInFirewallSetting : '' // This should contain ending ip in case IP range, else leave it as blank if no specific required ‘''if there is any single entry then allow all ips will not work due security reasons’ example: '10.245.*'
   databaseCollationNameDB:'SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS', // Default colation for the DB, you can use your preferred default SQL Server Collate and Coding style as per requirement ‘''to maintain a specific encoding format’ example: 'Default to Latin1 General (code page 1252) COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS'
   databaseCreateModeDB : "Single" // Single, Multiple or ExternalAccess depending on requirement. You can enable multi-model capability in future if needed at setup time using below parameter example: 'Multimodel Support enabled with multiple model capacity.' ‘externalaccess’ will be used to set this for test/dev environment
   databaseThresholdConnectTimeoutDB : "60" // Timeout (in seconds) before connection timeout error, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '28' example: ''5'' or more if there needs a specific time out duration ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseThresholdWaitTimeoutDB : "10" // Timeout (in seconds) before waiting timeout error, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '3' example: ''5'' or more if there needs a specific time out duration ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseThresholdIdleTimeoutDB : "60" // Timeout (in seconds) before connection timeout error, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '28' example: ''5'' or more if there needs a specific time out duration ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseMaxSizeMBDB : "10" // Maximum size for the DB data and log files, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '24' example: ''5'' or more if there needs a specific max storage capacity ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseBackupEnabledDB : true | false;// You can enable/disable backups at setup time using below parameter, by setting it to FALSE you do not want automatic backup feature. Default is 'True' example: ''False'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for back ups ‘example:'Backups disabled’
   databaseGeoBackupEnabledDB : true | false;// You can enable/disable geobackup at setup time using below parameter, by setting it to FALSE you do not want automatic Geo backup feature. Default is 'True' example: ''False'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for backups ‘example:'GeoBackup disabled’
   databasePauseTimeoutDB : "30" // Pause timeout duration (in minutes), used when auto-pausing on high load, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '6' example: ''5'' or more if there needs a specific time out for pause ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseReadReplicasDB : "1" // Read replicas count (number), used to handle high read traffic, you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is '0' example: ''3'' or more if there needs a specific number for reading from DB ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseSecondaryLocationDB : "West US" // The geo-replication secondary location (name), you can use your preferred required value in case of change. Default is 'uswest' example: ''east'' or more if there needs a specific name for read replicas ‘example:'more than default’
   databaseHighAvailabilityEnabledDB : true | false; // Enable/disable high availability at setup time using below parameter, by setting it to FALSE you do not want automatic High Avail feature. Default is 'True' example: ''False'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for HA backups ‘example:'HA backup disabled’
   databaseAllowEncryptedValueTransitDB : true | false; // Enable/disable allow encrypted value transit at setup time using below parameter, by setting it to FALSE you do not want automatic encryption feature. Default is 'True' example: ''False'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for data in transition ‘example:'Encryption disabled’

   databaseSingleWriteRegionDB : true | false; // Enable/disable single write region at setup time using below parameter, by setting it to FALSE you do not want automatic Single Write Region backup mode enabled in DB when data is written. Default is 'False' example: ''True'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for SWR ‘example:'SingleWriteRegion disabled’

Default is 'False' example: ''True'' or more if there needs a specific storage location for LTR ‘example:'LongTermRetention disabled’
